---
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    mainfont: Candara
    papersize: a4
    fontsize: 10pt
    geometry: 
      - left=15mm
      - right=15mm
      - top=20mm
      - bottom=20mm
    numbersections: false
    urlcolor: "blue"
    link-citations: true
---


# SUITABILITY STATEMENT

**Senior Lecturer in Civil Engineering (Environment and Sustainability) — Academic Level C**
College of Science and Engineering — Flinders University

*André Luiz Barbosa Nunes da Cunha, PhD | alcunha@usp.br*

---

## 1. PhD in Civil or Environmental Engineering

I hold a **PhD in Transportation Engineering** (2013) and an **MSc in Transportation Engineering** (2007), both from the University of São Paulo (USP), ranked among the top engineering schools in Latin America. My doctoral thesis, *"Automatic system for vehicular traffic parameters using OpenCV,"* pioneered the application of computer vision to transport infrastructure monitoring—a research stream I have since expanded into deep learning, network resilience, and sustainable infrastructure. My BSc in Civil Engineering (Federal University of Mato Grosso do Sul, 2004) was awarded with Highest Honours (GPA 3.79/4.00). Transport engineering is a core sub-discipline of civil engineering, and my work directly addresses the environmental and sustainability dimensions of infrastructure systems, including pavement decarbonisation, urban climate resilience, and emissions modelling.

## 2. Significant Teaching Track Record and Coordination

Since 2014, I have held a **tenured faculty position at USP**, where I have been solely responsible for the coordination, design, delivery, and assessment of multiple undergraduate and postgraduate topics. My teaching portfolio spans six distinct courses across data science, transport simulation, logistics, and infrastructure. I have served as **topic coordinator** for all of these, managing cohorts of 50+ students per semester, and have contributed to broader **course coordination** through my elected membership on the Graduate Program Coordinating Committee (CCP-ET), where I oversee admissions, curriculum quality, and scholarship allocation for both MSc and PhD cohorts.

My curriculum design contributions include: (i) **creating a new elective** in Computational Tools for Civil Engineering, introducing 20+ students annually to programming, algorithmic problem-solving, AutoCAD, Civil 3D, and GIS; (ii) **modernising the graduate curriculum** in Multivariate Data Analysis to include AI techniques (neural networks, decision trees, genetic algorithms) and R programming; and (iii) **completely redesigning** the Logistics and Supply Chain Management syllabus to integrate AI-driven logistics, GIS route planning, and Green Logistics best practices. My approach to civil and environmental engineering education consistently links technical skills to sustainability outcomes, equipping graduates to address real-world infrastructure challenges.

## 3. Delivering Civil/Environmental Engineering and Sustainability Topics

I am well positioned to deliver a broad range of topics in civil and environmental engineering with a sustainability focus. My current teaching covers both foundational courses (Fundamentals of Transportation Engineering) and advanced simulation topics (Traffic Engineering and Simulation using VISSIM, AIMSUN, and SUMO), all of which I frame within the context of sustainable urban mobility, emissions reduction, and climate-resilient infrastructure. My research agenda directly feeds into my teaching: doctoral and masters students I supervise work on **pavement recycling and decarbonisation** (FAPESP, ~USD 1.45M), **urban flood vulnerability** analysis using graph theory, and **emissions estimation models** for Brazilian transport networks—all of which generate datasets, case studies, and methodological innovations that I bring into the classroom.

At Flinders, I would be ready to contribute to topics in transport engineering, infrastructure sustainability, environmental impact assessment, computational methods, and data-driven engineering. I am also prepared to teach broadly across introductory engineering courses given my strong foundation across the civil engineering discipline.

## 4. Supervision of Honours and Higher Degree Students to Completion

I have an extensive and successful supervision record, having directed **60+ researchers** across all academic levels: **3 PhD completions** (with 3 in progress), **12 MSc completions** (3 in progress), 16 scientific initiation projects, and 25 capstone projects. My completed doctoral students have defended theses on deep learning for truck classification (Marcomini, 2025), flood vulnerability analysis using graph theory (Morelli, 2025), and operational safety of heavy vehicles. MSc graduates have published in journals including *Transportation Research Part D*, *Urban Informatics*, and *Transportes*, and have been placed at national regulatory agencies (ANTT, ARTESP), leading consultancies, and the financial sector. I take a structured mentorship approach, guiding students from research formulation through to publication and professional development.

## 5. Strong Research Track Record: Publications and Grants

My research programme bridges **transport modelling, AI/data science, and infrastructure sustainability**. I have authored **20 peer-reviewed journal articles** (including publications in *Transportation Research Part D*, *Transportation*, *Travel Behaviour and Society*, and *Urban Informatics*) and **36 refereed conference proceedings**. Three manuscripts are currently under review at high-impact journals (*IEEE TVCG*, *Transport Reviews*, *Journal of Urban Mobility*).

I have secured approximately **USD 3.38 million (~BRL 18.6M)** in total research funding, including: (i) Co-PI on a FAPESP-funded **Centers for Science for Development grant (USD 1.45M, 2025–2030)** on pavement recycling as a pillar of decarbonisation—directly aligned with Flinders' sustainability mission; (ii) Research Collaborator on the **CEPID "IARA" programme (USD 1.8M)** on AI for autonomous environments; and (iii) PI on multiple industry contracts with CCR Group and ARTERIS, where I developed national safety standards for truck escape ramps on federal highways. This blend of competitive peer-reviewed grants and translational industry contracts demonstrates my capacity to attract funding from diverse sources and deliver research with real-world impact.

## 6. Nexus Between Teaching, Learning, Research, and Scholarship

I actively integrate research into my teaching practice. In my graduate course on Multivariate Data Analysis, students work with real datasets generated by my research group, and several have gone on to co-author publications based on their coursework. My "inverted classroom" approach in Fundamentals of Transportation Engineering—where students engage with pre-recorded content before applying concepts in supervised, problem-based sessions—achieved student evaluations of **4.5/5.0** and was recognised with two consecutive **"Best Professor" awards** (2016, 2017) from the Civil Engineering student body. Undergraduate capstone projects (25+ supervised) are systematically linked to active research themes, ensuring that teaching and research outputs are mutually reinforcing. This commitment to research-led education aligns directly with the scholarly teaching culture Flinders promotes.

## 7. Leadership in Curriculum Reviews and Accreditation

I contribute to institutional governance and curriculum stewardship at multiple levels. As an **elected member of the Department Council**, I participate in decisions on faculty hiring priorities, budget allocation, and strategic partnerships. Through the **Graduate Program Coordinating Committee (CCP-ET)**, I directly influence curriculum quality control, admissions standards, and programme accreditation. I also serve on the **Center for Educational Technology (CETEPE)** and the **Culture & Extension Committee (CCEx)**, driving pedagogical modernisation across the engineering school. These governance roles have given me substantial experience with curriculum review cycles and quality assurance frameworks that would transfer directly to Flinders' internal and external accreditation processes, including Engineers Australia accreditation requirements.

## 8. Communication Skills and National/International Relationships

I have built a strong international network through visiting appointments at the **University of Melbourne** (Australia, 2020), **Technical University of Munich** (Germany, 2016–2017), **University of Zagreb** (Croatia, 2018, 2022), and the **University of Minho** (Portugal, 2017). These collaborations have produced joint publications, co-supervised students, and co-funded grants (including ERASMUS+ and FAPESP-BAYLAT). On the industry side, I maintain strategic partnerships with major Brazilian highway concessionaires (CCR Group, ARTERIS), where my simulation work has directly influenced national safety standards—work that was featured on Brazil's leading national news programme (Rede Globo, *Jornal Hoje*). I serve as a peer reviewer for Q1 journals (*Transportation Research Part E*, *Engineering Applications of AI*) and on technical committees for TRB, IEEE ITSS, and ANPET. My existing familiarity with the Australian academic system through my Melbourne appointment positions me for a productive transition to Flinders.

## 9. Experience and Innovation with E-Learning Platforms

I have extensive experience with **Moodle** as the primary learning management system at USP (*e-Disciplinas*), where I design and deliver blended learning environments for all my courses. My use of the platform goes beyond content hosting: I create structured learning pathways with sequenced modules, automated quizzes with instant feedback, discussion forums for peer learning, and submission workflows with rubric-based assessment. During the COVID-19 pandemic, I rapidly transitioned all courses to fully online delivery, combining Moodle with synchronous video sessions and asynchronous project-based tasks. My pedagogical innovation is further evidenced by my "inverted classroom" methodology, where I produce pre-recorded video lectures and interactive problem sets that students engage with before in-person sessions focused on application and deeper analysis. I am a board member of USP's **Center for Educational Technology (CETEPE)**, contributing to institution-wide e-learning strategy and digital pedagogy standards. I am confident in my ability to adopt Flinders' digital platforms (such as Canvas) and to contribute innovative, technology-enhanced teaching practices to the College.

---

I am enthusiastic about the opportunity to join Flinders University's College of Science and Engineering and to contribute to its strategic growth in civil and environmental engineering. My research in sustainable transport infrastructure, urban climate resilience, and AI-driven engineering solutions—combined with my demonstrated commitment to curriculum innovation and student mentorship—positions me to make a meaningful impact at Flinders. I welcome the opportunity to discuss how my experience can complement the work of colleagues such as Dr. Gholampour in sustainable construction materials, and to help advance Flinders' mission in engineering education and sustainability research.
